title: Inconsistent Index Origin in list_transform() and list_reduce()
description:
### What happens?

In a nutshell, list_transform() uses 1 as its index-origin (IO=1)
whereas list_reduce() uses 0 instead:
```
D select list_transform([10,20,30], (x,ix) -> ix) as ix;
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ    ix     ‚îÇ
‚îÇ  int64[]  ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ [1, 2, 3] ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò

D select list_reduce([10,20,30], (acc,x,ix) -> ix) as ix;
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ  ix   ‚îÇ
‚îÇ int32 ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ     2 ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```


### To Reproduce

```
select list_transform([10,20,30], (x,ix) -> ix) as ix;
select list_reduce([10,20,30], (acc,x,ix) -> ix) as ix;
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.3-dev75 c73cfca650 et al

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Peter Koppstein

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, values ARRAY<INT>);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, ARRAY[10, 20, 30]), (2, ARRAY[NULL, 40, 50]), (3, ARRAY[60, 70, NULL]);
SELECT id, ARRAY(SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1 FROM UNNEST(values)) AS transformed_ix FROM test_table;
SELECT id, MAX(ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1) AS reduced_ix FROM test_table WHERE values IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, ARRAY(SELECT x + 1 FROM UNNEST(values) AS x) AS incremented_values FROM test_table WHERE values IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Different index origins in list_transform() and list_reduce()
